_comment: TEMPLATE — copy to trackers/<your-id>.json and open index.html?id=<your-id>. Fields are grouped into camera / processing / communication; the _camera/_processing/_communication keys are just section headers and are ignored by the tracker. JSON has no real comments, so field docs live in _fields below.

_fields.tracker_id: human label for this tracker (informational; the real id is the ?id= in the URL / filename)
_fields.camera: which webcam: label substring like 'BRIO' or 'FaceTime' (preferred — stable across replugs), OR an exact deviceId, OR a numeric index (0,1,2…). Label matching is case-insensitive.
_fields.resolution: requested capture size { width, height }. The browser picks the closest the camera supports.
_fields.model: 'movenet' (17 keypoints, multi-person, 2D), 'mediapipe' (33 keypoints, 3D), 'yolov8' (17 keypoints, multi-person, 2D), or 'yolo11' (17 keypoints, multi-person, 2D)
_fields.confidence: 0..1 minimum keypoint score used for drawing
_fields.mirror: true = selfie/flip horizontally (raw video AND keypoints stay consistent)
_fields.calibration: RESERVED for later — path to a lens undistortion / calibration file (null for now)
_fields.collector: hub WebSocket URL, e.g. ws://localhost:9000 or ws://192.168.1.50:9000
_fields.streamSkeleton: true = send computed keypoints/skeleton frames to the collector (turn off to go dark / save bandwidth)
_fields.streamRaw: true = send throttled raw JPEG frames to the collector so it can show video behind the skeleton
